entries.description: Registered S1 via python -m ai_router.start_session (engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-8, effort=high). On-disk state had 4 sessions vs the spec's 5 (scope was broadened); passed --total-sessions 5 to reconcile (the 5th session was appended). Confirmed set NTE budget $10 via AskUserQuestion (matches Sets 047/048/049).

Inventoried both features' composition points via two Explore agents. Feature 1: needsMigration detector (fileSystem.ts ~L547-564), the two migrator CLIs (migrate_v3_to_v4 .v3.bak.json, migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4 .lwbak.json), normalize_to_v4_shape shim (progress.py:312), SCHEMA_VERSION_V4 constant (progress.py:31, re-exported as SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION), the existing claude-session-start-invoker.js + installOrchestratorHook.claudeCode command, cross-repo notice blob-URL pattern. Feature 2: trigger phrase (rowMenuHelpers.ts:85 / copyPromptCommands.ts), copy* commands incl. copySlug, slug=directory basename (fileSystem.ts:699), ActionRegistry flat actions, authoring-guide slug section (discourages number prefixes), no existing prefix-parsing.

Confirmed RouteResult exposes model_name/model_id/tier but NO .provider attribute (the recurring crash trap from prior sets). Chose to call providers.call_model directly with config=cfg['providers'][provider_name], avoiding route() and the trap entirely. Identified consensus engines: gemini-pro (google/gemini-2.5-pro), gpt-5-4 (openai/gpt-5.4) with gpt-5-4-mini fallback.
Authored proposal.md with recommended dispositions on all 11 open questions (7 Feature-1, 4 Feature-2) plus rationale, carried risks, and S2-S5 scope.

Ran devil's-advocate two-pass consensus: Pass A (evaluate each disposition) + Pass B (devil's advocate challenging the author's minimal-surface bias) against gemini-pro and gpt-5-4. All four calls succeeded (no 429 cascade). Total $0.2829 (gemini $0.0128+$0.0226; gpt-5-4 $0.1167+$0.1309). Convergent direction; 8 of 11 dispositions flipped. Biggest correction (gpt-5-4 pass-b): the SessionStart drift scan must be pure-JS / no-router because the incident repo had an ancient router with no check_migrations.

Wrote verdict.md (locked dispositions, net-change table, carried risks, S2-S5 scope-lock). Journaled the consensus to ai_router/consensus-decisions.jsonl (category spec-clarification, agreement partial). Scope-locked spec.md: audit-locked banner over the open-questions section, refined S2 (declarative manifest + detect-only CLI), S3 (pure-JS hot-path scan + CI constant-equality test), S4 (two-surface resolver), deliverables, and the --apply non-goal.

Authored docs/schema-current.json per the S1-locked Q3 schema: declarative only (manifestVersion 1, currentSchemaVersion 4, minimumAiRouterVersion 0.10.0, raw-on-master schemaDocUrl, blob migrationGuideUrl) with migrators as symbolic IDs + version ranges (NO executable shell strings). Migrators array carries three entries (v2-to-v3, lightweight-to-v4, v3-to-v4) — see step 4 for why the verdict's two-entry example was corrected.

Implemented ai_router/check_migrations.py (detect-only). Scans <scan>/*/session-state.json (reuses discover_session_sets from migrate_v3_to_v4), classifies each as clean/drift/ahead/unreadable against LOCAL_SCHEMA_VERSION (= session_state.SCHEMA_VERSION). Terse one-line default, --verbose/--json detail, exit non-zero on drift (--exit-zero suppresses). Optional --manifest-url advisory fetch (fail-open + ~/.dabbler cache, injectable fetch_fn for tests); --strict-manifest flips to fail-loud. Migrator-ID→module map + ordered bulk chain live in local code (manifest stays declarative). ASCII-only output (Windows cp1252 console crashes on non-ASCII, empirically confirmed). Relative-import fallback per the Set 048 bare-import lesson. No pyproject entry-point needed — sibling migrators also use python -m invocation.

Wrote ai_router/tests/test_check_migrations.py (21 tests): clean repo, mixed v2/v3/v4/missing-version classification, corrupt/non-object unreadable (non-fatal), ahead-file, bool-not-int guard, manifest success+cache-fallback+no-cache+strict-raises, manifest==SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION CI guard, migrator-IDs-known, the carried-risk-#2 v2-needs-chain end-to-end test, bulk-command shape, and CLI exit-code/json/exit-zero/ascii-only/manifest-note cases. Full suite green: 988 passed, 1 skipped, 0 regressions. Real-CLI harvester-mirror (2 v2 among 3 v4) reports exit 1 with both v2 sets flagged and the corrected chain recommended.

DEVIATION FROM S1 VERDICT (flagged for S5 verifier + operator). Verdict Q7 locked the bulk-upgrade sequence as two migrators (lightweight-to-v4 then v3-to-v4) claiming it handles 'a v2 set that needs both steps'. The S2 carried-risk-#2 test empirically falsified this: a genuine v2 file (explicit schemaVersion:2 + legacy currentSession/totalSessions/completedSessions triple) is SKIPPED by both (lightweight→skipped-pre-v3, v3-to-v4→skipped-not-v3). The v2→v3 step is a THIRD existing migrator, migrate_session_state, which the verdict omitted. Corrected bulk chain (no new migrator logic — orchestrates an existing one): migrate_session_state → migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4 → migrate_v3_to_v4, each --in-place/idempotent; verified to take genuine v2 losslessly to v4. Documented in module docstring (CHAIN NOTE) + CHANGELOG; manifest migrators array reflects all three.

Implementation committed at b8b2066 (authored by a prior claude-sonnet-4-6 chat; that chat then backfilled the register-session-start at 3859066 four seconds later and stopped without closing out). Per the S1-locked S3 scope: (1) claude-session-start-invoker.js gains CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 4 and scanSchemaDrift(workspaceRoot) — reads docs/session-sets/*/session-state.json, compares each schemaVersion to the bundled constant, emits a terse one-line summary on drift (clean = silent), fail-open on unreadable/missing files; NO ai_router import, NO network, so it works when the router is absent or stale. Chained AFTER spawnStartSession in main() with start_session errors logged-not-fatal so the scan always runs. (2) installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts success toast updated to mention both start_session and the drift scan, plus a 'Copy manual setup' button copying the minimal invoker download URL + settings.json stanza for repos without the extension (works with no router installed). (3) test_invoker_schema_constant.py — CI guard reading CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION from the JS via regex and asserting == ai_router SCHEMA_VERSION (skips gracefully if the invoker isn't found). (4) ai-led-session-workflow.md documents the guard at Step 1. (5) 8 new Layer-2 tests in claudeSessionStartInvoker.test.ts for scanSchemaDrift + CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION.

Takeover/continuation by claude-opus-4-8: the prior chat left S3 in-progress with the deliverable committed+pushed but no activity-log entry, no S3 disposition, and no close-out. Critically reviewed the committed diff and confirmed it satisfies all four S1-locked S3 steps. Ran the verification: Python test_invoker_schema_constant.py + test_check_migrations.py green (22 passed); TS Layer-2 npm run test:unit shows 561 passing / 2 failing where BOTH failures (configEditor-foundation panel-lifecycle vscode.ViewColumn.One stub gap; notificationsSection stale 'Set 026' assertion) live in files last touched in commit c2a336f (Set 026) and are pre-existing stub-harness failures unrelated to S3 — the invoker's own scanSchemaDrift + CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION suite passes within the run. SCOPE FLAG for S4/S5 (carried forward): the end-of-set 'Explorer UX revision' deliverable (replace the (needs migration) row description with an asterisk + 'Ran under schema v<N>' tooltip; add a single title-bar 'Upgrade older session sets' icon running the corrected three-migrator bulk chain) appears in End-of-set deliverables but is NOT in any session's Steps — it is unassigned and was NOT done in S3 (S3's steps do not include it). It needs a home in S4 (next extension session) or S5. Cross-provider verification deferred to S5 per the established per-set pattern (S2 did the same). No router invoked; $0 added to the $10 set NTE.

S4 implemented Feature 2 (number-prefix addressing) AND the operator-reassigned Explorer UX revision. Feature 2 (verdict Q8-Q11): new ai_router/resolve_set.py exposes resolve_slug/resolve_set (exact integer-prefix match, leading zeros normalized, collision names both, no-match lists available numbers + --next; no fuzzy nearest), next_session_set_number (max(existing)+1 returned as int + zero-padded string, width=max(3, widest existing prefix)), resolve_session_set_dir (bare-number -> path; path passes through), and a standalone CLI (`python -m ai_router.resolve_set <n>` / `--next` / `--json`). start_session --session-set-dir now accepts a bare number ('50') that resolves within ./docs/session-sets. authoring guide reconciled: monotonic NNN- sequence prefix permitted (REQUIRED for new/scaffolded sets, RECOMMENDED + forward-only for consumer repos), semantic date/phase names still banned, new Numbering section documents max+1 + zero-pad width + no-mass-rename. Extension gained a minimal Command-Palette quick-input resolver command (dabblerSessionSets.resolveSetNumber) backed by a pure TS resolver (utils/resolveSetNumber.ts) mirroring the Python contract so a router-less Lightweight consumer still gets the handle. Explorer UX revision (operator-directed to S4; non-goal 'old schema is OK, no per-row nag'): the intrusive '(needs migration)' row description is replaced by an unobtrusive asterisk + 'Ran under schema v<N>' hover tooltip (SessionSetsModel.migrationMarker/migrationTooltip; schemaVersionOnDisk surfaced from fileSystem.ts; rendered in client.js + tree.css .row-migration-marker). A single title-bar 'Upgrade older session sets' icon (commands/upgradeOlderSets.ts), gated on the dabblerSessionSets.hasSubCurrentSets context key (set during scan from the pure hasSubCurrentSets predicate), runs the corrected THREE-migrator bulk chain (migrate_session_state -> migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4 -> migrate_v3_to_v4, each --in-place via Python subprocess) across all sub-current sets at once -- the S2 empirical correction, NOT the verdict's two. The per-row right-click 'Migrate to v3/v4 schema' actions are left intact (a manual option, not a standing nag). Verification (manual; cross-provider deferred to S5 per the per-set pattern S2/S3 followed): Python `python -m pytest` = 1025 passed / 1 skipped (15 new resolver tests + 2 new start_session integration tests, 0 regressions); TS `npm run test:unit` = 573 passing / 2 failing where BOTH failures (configEditor-foundation panel-lifecycle + notificationsSection stale Set-026 assertion) are pre-existing in files last touched in Set 026 commit c2a336f and unrelated to S4; tsc --noEmit clean; esbuild bundle clean; the new resolveSetNumber + migrationMarker suites pass. Layer-3 Playwright migration-cta specs updated to assert the asterisk + tooltip (not run locally -- Layer-3 launchVSCode is CI-fragile/slow on this host; the title-bar enabled/disabled gating is unit-tested via hasSubCurrentSets since the package.json `when` clause is declarative). No router invoked; $0 added to the $10 set NTE.

Wrote docs/cross-repo-migration-guard-notice.md (paste-in for consumer CLAUDE.md): one-time setup (install the SessionStart drift guard via the extension command OR the router-less manual fallback -- invoker download URL + minimal settings.json stanza; raise the >=0.10.0 pin; run a one-time check_migrations + bulk-upgrade-on-drift), a self-contained copy-snippet (machine-enforced schema currency, raw GitHub URLs not blob, schemaVersion:4 stamp, omit-null, old-schema-is-OK no-nag posture, NNN- number-prefix addressing with resolve_set/--next), required-agent caveats, and a 2026-05-29 adoption-status table. Verified the referenced CLIs behave as documented: resolve_set --next -> 053, resolve_set 50 -> full slug, check_migrations exits 1 on older-schema drift (corrected an earlier mis-read: the `exit: 0` I first saw was head's exit code through a pipe, not python's -- real default exit is 1, --exit-zero forces 0).

Confirmed harvester first-adopter status. dabbler-access-harvester: all 49 session sets now at schemaVersion 4 (0 needs-migration), router pin dabbler-ai-router>=0.10.0 in requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md carries the inlined 'Stamp schemaVersion:4 on every write' rule + raw-URL fetch guidance -- all done 2026-05-29 ahead of this set (the immediate incident unblock). The remaining adopter action is the one-time SessionStart hook install; because the hook lives in the SHARED GLOBAL ~/.claude/settings.json (the invoker walks up from cwd to find the in-progress set), installing it once covers the harvester and every other repo on the machine at once -- an operator-run machine-level action, not a per-repo commit. Other consumer repos' CLAUDE.md snippet paste-in remains operator-scoped per established cross-repo pattern.

Dual version bumps + changelogs. dabbler-ai-router 0.11.0 -> 0.12.0 (pyproject.toml + ai_router/__init__.__version__); extension 0.24.1 -> 0.25.0 (package.json). ai_router/CHANGELOG.md: cut a [0.12.0] release header capturing S4 resolve_set (Feature 2) + the S2 check_migrations/manifest content (moved out of [Unreleased]). tools/.../CHANGELOG.md: new [0.25.0] entry (pure-JS drift scan + install path, resolveSetNumber command, upgradeOlderSets title-bar icon, Explorer asterisk/tooltip no-nag Change, plus the now-published held 0.24.1 Copy-Slug Fixed). CLAUDE.md Extension versioning: new Current v0.25.0 (Set 050) block; cascaded the Previous/Pre-Previous chain down one level each (0.24.1 -> Previous, 0.24.0 -> Pre-Previous, 0.23.0/0.22.0/0.21.0/0.18.1 each +1 'Pre-'). Created set-level change-log.md (per-session summary, commit hashes, costs). Full Python suite re-run after bumps: 1025 passed / 1 skipped / 0 regressions. No TS source changed in S5 (version+CHANGELOG only), so no rebuild needed -- S4 already confirmed tsc/esbuild clean.

Cross-provider IV&V of the S2-S5 deferred work (deferred to S5 by S2/S3/S4 per the per-set pattern). Verifier gemini-pro (google) -- different provider from the Claude orchestrator. Called providers.call_model directly with the PROVIDER-SCOPED config (cfg['providers']['google']) -- the full cfg lacks a top-level 'retry' key (it is nested per-provider), and this also sidesteps the recurring RouteResult.provider attribute trap. First call with max_tokens=4000 returned a candidate with no text parts (gemini-2.5-pro dynamic thinking consumed the whole budget -> KeyError 'parts'); fixed by max_tokens=16000 + thinking_budget=4096. Verdict VERIFIED: 0 critical; 2 important (both NOTED RISKS not defects: (a) three-step bulk migration safety relies on each migrator's idempotency -- already a documented/test-asserted invariant; (b) Python/TS resolver dual-impl drift risk -- acknowledged, convention-lint a future-set candidate); 1 nice-to-have (the 2 pre-existing Set-026 TS failures -- out of scope, predate this set). The verifier explicitly confirmed claim 2 (the empirically-corrected THREE-migrator chain vs the verdict's two) as 'a sound and empirically-validated correction' and called release discipline sound. No in-flight fix required. Record at s5-verification.md. Cost $0.0053 -> cumulative routed $0.2882/$10 NTE (2.9%).
